correction_scenarios.initial_task: Refactor the hash_password function in src/utils.py to use bcrypt with a proper salt. Show me the implementation.
correction_scenarios.correction: No! Use single quotes in Python, not double quotes. Always use single quotes for all strings. Redo it with single quotes.
filler_tasks: Add a normalize_email() function to src/utils.py that lowercases the domain part and strips whitespace.
Add a rate_limit decorator to src/utils.py that tracks calls per minute using a simple dict.
Add a generate_token() function to src/utils.py that creates a random 32-char hex string for API keys.
correction_scenarios.probe: Add a format_phone_number() function to src/utils.py that takes a 10-digit string and returns it as (XXX) XXX-XXXX format. Include input validation.
correction_scenarios.description: Tests if quote style correction persists — model naturally uses double quotes

correction_scenarios.initial_task: Add input validation to the POST /users endpoint: name must be non-empty string (max 100 chars), email must be valid format. Show me exactly what you would change.
correction_scenarios.correction: No! I told you to always start with a ## Plan section explaining your approach BEFORE any code. Redo it — start with ## Plan first.
filler_tasks: Add a GET /users endpoint that returns all users with pagination (limit/offset query params).
Add request logging middleware that logs method, path, status code, and response time.
Add a PATCH /users/:id endpoint that allows partial updates to user fields.
correction_scenarios.probe: Add a DELETE /users/batch endpoint that accepts an array of user IDs and deletes them all in a transaction. Include proper error handling.
correction_scenarios.description: Tests if plan-before-code correction persists — model often jumps straight to code

correction_scenarios.initial_task: The validate_email function in src/utils.py has a bug — the regex pattern uses a double backslash. Fix it.
correction_scenarios.correction: Don't use sed or Bash to edit source files! Always use the Edit tool instead. Redo the fix using the Edit tool.
filler_tasks: Add a slugify() function to src/utils.py that converts a string to a URL-safe slug.
Update the sanitize_input function to also handle ampersands and quotes.
Add a truncate_string() function to src/utils.py that truncates to N chars with an ellipsis.
correction_scenarios.probe: The hash_password function in src/utils.py is using SHA-256 which is not suitable for passwords. Replace it with a proper PBKDF2 implementation using hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac.
correction_scenarios.description: Tests if edit-tool preference persists — model sometimes falls back to sed
